description: Implement a DIMACS min-cost-flow format parser that produces []Arc with uint256 capacities.
steps: Implement `func ParseDIMACS(r io.Reader) (arcs []Arc, n int, src int, sink int, err error)`. Support `c` comments, `p min <N> <M>`, `n <ID> <SUPPLY>`, `a <U> <V> <LOW> <CAP> <COST>`.

Convert 1-indexed file node IDs to 0-indexed. src is the node with positive supply; sink is the node with negative supply. Multiple sources or sinks → error. LOW != 0 → error ('non-zero lower bounds unsupported').
Parse CAP via `new(uint256.Int).SetFromDecimal(capStr)`; parse COST via `strconv.ParseInt(s, 10, 64)`. Append `Arc{Source: u-1, Target: v-1, Cost: cost, Capacity: cap256, Flow: uint256.NewInt(0)}`.
Create dimacs_test.go. `TestParseDIMACS_Inline`: inline 5-node fixture with 6 arcs and at least one `c` comment; assert n==5, len(arcs)==6, src+sink indices, one arc's Cost/Capacity/Source/Target.
`TestParseDIMACS_Errors`: subtests for missing `p` line, two positive-supply nodes, non-zero LOW. Each returns a non-nil error.
acceptance_criteria: dimacs.go exports `ParseDIMACS(io.Reader) ([]Arc, int, int, int, error)`.
